title: Rail Fence Cipher Part 1: Encoding
body:
The Rail Fence Cipher hides a message by writing its letters in a zig-zag pattern across several “rails” or horizontal rows. Once written out in this pattern, the final encoded message is created by reading off the letters row by row. 
In this lesson, you’ll practise encoding words and short phrases using a fixed number of rails. Focus on how the letters move down and up across the rails as you write them — the rhythm of this movement is the key to getting the pattern right. 
Example: encoding the text WE ARE DISCOVERED RUN AT ONCE results in the ciphertext WECRUOERDSOEERNTNEAIVDAC. 
##railfencecipher.png##
lesson: CRYPTO-D2A-2bba0d31, CRYPTO-D2B-1a0e7d92, CRYPTO-D3A-6d4c8a11, CRYPTO-D3B-3f1e9b74, CRYPTO-D6A-c1f7d283, CRYPTO-D6B-0ea984c5
